name: Section
description: A complete unit of narrative prose ending in one or more player-facing choices. Sections are the atomic reading units of the manuscript, written by Scene Smith from Section Briefs. In Cold state, sections are player-safe and ready for export.

fields.name: brief_ref
fields.description: Reference to the Section Brief this implements (format: SB-NNN)

fields.name: prose
fields.description: Narrative paragraphs with opening beat, development, and setup for choices. 150-500 words typical. May contain inline codex references as bold text.

fields.name: choices
fields.description: Player-facing choices at the end of the section. Minimum 2, maximum 5.

properties.name: text
properties.description: Choice text shown to player (concrete verb + object)

properties.name: target_anchor
properties.description: Anchor ID this choice leads to

properties.name: condition_type
properties.description: Type of gate: token, reputation, knowledge, tool

properties.name: diegetic_check
properties.description: In-world phrasing for the gate (what PN says)

properties.name: diegetic_refusal
properties.description: In-world phrasing when gate blocks access

fields.name: codex_refs
fields.description: List of codex entry IDs referenced in this section

properties.name: art_plan_ref
properties.description: Reference to Art Plan if exists

properties.name: audio_plan_ref
properties.description: Reference to Audio Plan if exists

fields.name: convergence_notes
fields.description: If this section converges from multiple paths, notes for immediate reflection

states.name: Draft
states.description: Section is being written by Scene Smith

states.name: Review
states.description: Section is under Style Lead review

states.name: Approved
states.description: Section has passed gatecheck, ready for Cold merge

states.name: Cold
states.description: Section is merged to Cold SoT, player-safe

custom_rules.description: Choices must be contrastive (different outcomes), not synonyms
No internals, codewords, gate logic, or spoilers in prose or choice text
All gates must use in-world phrasing (token, reputation, knowledge, tool)
All target anchors must resolve to valid sections
